chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Has Been Reported Regarding Looking at Women

text: 4972 - وَ قَالَ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ لِلصَّادِقِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : اَلرَّجُلُ تَمُرُّ بِهِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ فَيَنْظُرُ إِلَى خَلْفِهَا قَالَ "أَ يَسُرُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يُنْظَرَ إِلَى أَهْلِهِ وَ ذَاتِ قَرَابَتِهِ" قُلْتُ لاَ قَالَ "فَارْضَ لِلنَّاسِ مَا تَرْضَاهُ لِنَفْسِكَ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.4972 - Abu Basir asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) about a man who looks at a woman passing by and then turns to look at her from behind.
Imam (as) replied: "Would any of you like someone to look at his family or close female relatives?"
I said: "No."
Imam (as) said: "Then be pleased for others what you are pleased with for yourself."
